_comment_npm_registry: OPTIONAL upstream npm registry for the Node-based AI CLI. Microsoft-managed devices hard-block the public registries; packages must come through a CFS-protected feed. Leave empty to defer to the org-managed global ~/.npmrc (recommended). Set to https://packagefeedproxy.microsoft.io/npm/ if a machine's ambient config is missing and the CLI hard-fails.
codebase_description: a TypeScript / React / Node monorepo for <product name and what it does>
review_focus: API contracts between the frontend and backend — flag any drift
Telemetry: ensure new error paths emit a corresponding warning event
reviewer_style: Be direct and senior-engineer terse. Prefer one concrete fix over three vague suggestions. Never ask the author to 'consider' something without saying what and why.
_comment_flag_core_functionality_changes: When true, the reviewer estimates how much of the app's core functionality (see core_functionality_description) a PR changes; it forces request_changes and labels the PR '🚩 needs human review' ONLY when that estimate is >= core_functionality_block_threshold_pct. Lower-impact / hidden core changes decide normally (and may be auto-approved). Off by default.
core_functionality_description: the product's core / main-page functionality — anything prominent on the main page and primary surfaces users interact with directly — plus core business logic, critical execution paths, public API / interface contracts, data models or schemas, persistence, and authentication / authorization
_comment_auto_approve: When false, the bot never posts an APPROVE review — an approve decision is submitted as a non-blocking COMMENT so you approve PRs manually. Default true preserves auto-approve.
_comment_self_improve: When true, at the end of each cycle the tool reads OTHER reviewers' comments on the in-scope PRs, asks the model for generalizable improvements to its own reviewer prompt, and appends them to learned_guidance below (and to the memory/ wiki). Off by default. Force a one-off run with `python auto_review.py --learn`.
_comment_learned_guidance: Managed by the tool's self-improvement step — you normally don't hand-edit this. Each item: {kind: focus|avoid, category, text, rationale, learned_at}. Injected into the review prompt on later cycles.
